title: mimulus guttatus subsp. arvensis
intro: Mimulus guttatus subsp. arvensis, also known as the common yellow monkey flower, is a plant species native to Europe and North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive shape. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations. The health benefits of Mimulus guttatus subsp. arvensis include its potential to soothe anxiety, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing users to harness its medicinal properties in a convenient and accessible way.
